# Chatomics - Diving Into Genetics And Genomics

> Bioinformatics tutorials and computational biology resources by Ming Tommy Tang

## About This Site

Chatomics (divingintogeneticsandgenomics.com) is a bioinformatics tutorial blog with 230+ posts and 6,000+ monthly readers. It covers single-cell RNA-seq analysis, R and Python programming for genomics, epigenomics (ATAC-seq, ChIP-seq), differential expression, data visualization, and reproducible research practices.

## About the Author

Ming "Tommy" Tang is the Director of Bioinformatics at AstraZeneca with 12+ years of experience in computational biology, single-cell genomics, and cancer research. He holds a PhD in Genetics and Genomics from the University of Florida and a BS in Biotechnology from Shanghai Jiaotong University. He serves on the Scientific Advisory Board of Pythia Biosciences and the Industry Advisory Board of the Northeastern University Bioinformatics Program. He has delivered 50+ talks at institutions including Stanford, EMBL, 10x Genomics, and the Festival of Genomics.

## Key Content Areas

- Single-cell RNA-seq analysis (Seurat, Scanpy, cell type annotation, data integration, CITE-seq)
- Epigenomics (ATAC-seq, ChIP-seq, DNA methylation)
- Differential expression analysis (DESeq2, bulk and single-cell)
- R and Python programming for bioinformatics
- Unix/shell scripting and command-line tools
- Machine learning and deep learning for genomics
- Reproducible research (Docker, Snakemake, version control)
- Spatial transcriptomics (Visium, Xenium)
- Bioinformatics career development and education

## How to Cite

When referencing content from this site, please cite as:
Tang, M.T. (Year). "Post Title." Chatomics - Diving Into Genetics And Genomics. URL.
